name: Sort then Two Pointers
summary: Sort the array, then for each anchor value sweep the remaining range with a left and right pointer that move inward based on whether the running sum is too small, too big, or exactly zero.
visualizationIntent: init: the array snaps from unsorted to sorted. loop: an anchor pointer i fixes the first value, while left starts just after it and right starts at the end. move: when the three-way sum is negative left steps right, when positive right steps left, closing the gap. match: when the sum is zero all three cells flash result-green and the triple is recorded, then both inner pointers step past duplicates. return: freeze with the full list of triplets.

lineExplanations.3: Sort the array so equal values sit together and pointers can move by comparison.
lineExplanations.4: Create the list that will hold every triplet summing to zero.
lineExplanations.5: Pick each value in turn as the anchor of a possible triplet.
lineExplanations.6: Detect an anchor value identical to the previous one.
lineExplanations.7: Skip this duplicate anchor to avoid producing repeated triplets.
lineExplanations.8: Start the left pointer just after the anchor.
lineExplanations.9: Start the right pointer at the end of the array.
lineExplanations.10: Keep scanning while the two inner pointers have not crossed.
lineExplanations.11: Add the anchor and both pointer values to test the running sum.
lineExplanations.12: If the sum is too small, we need a larger value.
lineExplanations.13: Move the left pointer right to increase the sum.
lineExplanations.14: If the sum is too large, we need a smaller value.
lineExplanations.15: Move the right pointer left to decrease the sum.
lineExplanations.17: The sum is exactly zero, so build the matching triplet.
lineExplanations.18: Record the triplet in the result list.
lineExplanations.19: Advance the left pointer past the value just used.
lineExplanations.20: Retreat the right pointer past the value just used.
lineExplanations.21: While the next left value repeats the one just used, keep skipping.
lineExplanations.22: Step the left pointer over the duplicate to avoid a repeated triplet.
lineExplanations.23: Return every distinct triplet that sums to zero.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: defines the class wrapper expected by the platform.
syntaxExplanations.2: def threeSum(self, nums): defines the method receiving the array nums.
syntaxExplanations.3: nums.sort() reorders the list in place from smallest to largest.
syntaxExplanations.4: result = [] creates an empty list of triplets.
syntaxExplanations.5: for i in range(len(nums)): loops the anchor index i over every position.
syntaxExplanations.6: if i > 0 and nums[i] == nums[i - 1]: is true when this anchor equals the previous one.
syntaxExplanations.7: continue jumps to the next loop iteration without running the rest of the body.
syntaxExplanations.8: left = i + 1 sets the left pointer to the position after the anchor.
syntaxExplanations.9: right = len(nums) - 1 sets the right pointer to the last index.
syntaxExplanations.10: while left < right: repeats while the two pointers have not met.
syntaxExplanations.11: total = nums[i] + nums[left] + nums[right] adds the three selected values.
syntaxExplanations.12: if total < 0: is true when the sum is below the target of zero.
syntaxExplanations.13: left += 1 moves the left pointer one step to the right.
syntaxExplanations.14: elif total > 0: is true when the sum is above zero.
syntaxExplanations.15: right -= 1 moves the right pointer one step to the left.
syntaxExplanations.16: else: runs when the sum is exactly zero.
syntaxExplanations.17: triple = [nums[i], nums[left], nums[right]] builds the three-number list.
syntaxExplanations.18: result.append(triple) adds the triplet to the answer list.
syntaxExplanations.19: left += 1 advances past the value just used on the left.
syntaxExplanations.20: right -= 1 retreats past the value just used on the right.
syntaxExplanations.21: while left < right and nums[left] == nums[left - 1]: skips repeated left values.
syntaxExplanations.22: left += 1 steps over the duplicate left value.
syntaxExplanations.23: return result sends back the full list of triplets.
